scenario: Operate shows incidents but team wants to categorise -- input errors, network failures, business errors. Wonders incident classification.
question: Incident classifications / types?

options.text: mdx:113-130 -- 16 documented values: JOB_NO_RETRIES (worker exhausted retries), EXTRACT_VALUE_ERROR (FEEL expression evaluation failed), CONDITION_ERROR (gateway condition error), IO_MAPPING_ERROR (input/output mapping failed), DECISION_EVALUATION_ERROR (DMN decision failed), CALLED_DECISION_ERROR, CALLED_ELEMENT_ERROR, FORM_NOT_FOUND, UNHANDLED_ERROR_EVENT (BPMN error not caught), MESSAGE_SIZE_EXCEEDED, RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND, EXECUTION_LISTENER_NO_RETRIES, TASK_LISTENER_NO_RETRIES, AD_HOC_SUB_PROCESS_NO_RETRIES, UNKNOWN, and UNSPECIFIED. Categorise via the errorType field plus free-text errorMessage for detailed incident triage and routing.

Manual categorisation -- this is only partially applicable. mdx:113-130) with 16 distinct values. These cover categories like JOB_NO_RETRIES (worker failures), EXTRACT_VALUE_ERROR (FEEL evaluation failures), IO_MAPPING_ERROR (variable mapping failures), and UNHANDLED_ERROR_EVENT (uncaught BPMN errors). Building a fully custom classification from scratch when the platform already provides structured type information would be redundant effort.
Each incident also carries a free-text errorMessage for additional context. These types are queryable via the API and visible in Operate, enabling programmatic incident routing and filtering.

a.text: Incorrect. Incident types in Camunda 8: Built-in incident types per apis-tools/orchestration-cluster-api-rest/specifications/get-incident.api.mdx:113-130 IncidentErrorTypeEnum: JOB_NO_RETRIES: Worker called job.fail() repeatedly. Retries exhausted (0). Most common type. EXTRACT_VALUE_ERROR: FEEL expression evaluation failed. E.g., null pointer in customer.tier. CONDITION_ERROR: Gateway condition expression error. Failed to evaluate. IO_MAPPING_ERROR: Input / Output Mapping failed. E.g., type mismatch, missing variable. UNHANDLED_ERROR_EVENT: BPMN error thrown. No matching Boundary Event. EXECUTION_LISTENER_NO_RETRIES: Execution Listener failed and exhausted retries. DECISION_EVALUATION_ERROR / CALLED_DECISION_ERROR: DMN evaluation failed or referenced decision unavailable. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND / FORM_NOT_FOUND / CALLED_ELEMENT_ERROR: Referenced process / decision / form / call-activity target missing. MESSAGE_SIZE_EXCEEDED / AD_HOC_SUB_PROCESS_NO_RETRIES: Message payload over limit / ad-hoc subprocess listener exhausted. TASK_LISTENER_NO_RETRIES: User Task Listener failed and exhausted retries. In Operate: Incident detail: type + error message. Filter by type: incidents of specific kind. Search by error: text search. Custom categorisation: Via error message content: Worker includes structured info in error message. Operate / dashboards parse / categorise. Via BPMN error codes: Per Set 13 Q59: structured error codes. INVALID_VAT_NUMBER, INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, etc. Routes to specific handlers. For dashboards: Group by incident type: `promql count by (incident_type) (zeebe_incidents_active) ` Trend: incident rate per type over time. For analysis: Pattern: top incident causes: List incidents grouped by error message. Identify common issues. Fix systematically. Pattern: per-process incident rate: Group by process definition. Spot problematic processes. For automation: Auto-classification: Regex match on error message. Assign category. Route to appropriate ops team. For root cause analysis: Trace incident: Process instance context. Variables at incident time. Stack trace (worker logs). Combine signals: Operate incident details. Worker logs (correlated). Cluster metrics. For SaaS: Console may show high-level incident summary. Operate for detail. Best practices: Use specific error codes: better than generic. Structured error messages: parseable. Classify: route to right team. Track trends: identify systemic issues. For audit: All incidents logged. Resolution tracked: when retried / resolved. For alerting: Per-type thresholds: alert on specific patterns. Sudden spike: investigate. For Webhook incidents: Auth failures: separate from process errors. Categorise: validation vs auth vs process. For RPA incidents: Bot failures: timeout, element-not-found, etc. Categorise: similar to other workers. Anti-patterns: Single generic error: hard to classify. No structured info: lost context. Ignoring incidents: pile up. For the scenario (categorise incidents): Approach: 1. Use built-in incident types as primary categories. 2. Custom error codes for business errors. 3. Dashboards: aggregate by type. 4. Tickets: categorise per type. For maturity model: Level 1: incidents tracked. Level 2: incidents categorised. Level 3: trends analysed. Level 4: prevention proactive. For "incident classifications" reporting: Weekly review: top incident types. Action items: fixes scheduled.

findings.message: option text contains its own verdict/rationale
